title: Check Assignment Requirements
summary: Read and understand what an assignment is asking before starting.
learnerPromise: You will be able to read an assignment carefully, identify what is being asked, and create a checklist of what you need to do before you begin.
whyItMatters: Many students lose points simply because they missed part of the instructions. Taking a few minutes to check requirements before starting helps you do the assignment correctly the first time.
realLifeUses: Checking a rubric before writing an essay to know how you will be graded
Reviewing all parts of a multi-step homework assignment before starting
Confirming formatting rules like font size and margins for a paper
Making sure you answer every question in a worksheet or packet
materialsNeeded: Paper or notes app for your checklist
result: A checklist of what the assignment requires, with key requirements highlighted
youWillLearn: How to identify the main task an assignment is asking for
How to spot formatting, length, or deadline requirements
How to create a checklist from the instructions
How to notice hidden requirements like citations or specific sources
miniChallenge: Pick an assignment you have due soon. Read the instructions carefully and create a checklist of every requirement you need to meet.
steps: Read the full assignment once from start to finish without starting the work.
Underline or highlight the main task and the due date.
Look for details like length, format, number of sources, or specific topics.
Write a checklist of every requirement you need to meet.
Check off each item as you complete it while doing the assignment.
completionCriteria: You can state the main task of the assignment in one sentence
You have a written checklist of all requirements
You spotted at least one detail you might have missed on a quick read
commonProblems: Starting the work before fully reading the instructions
Missing requirements that are listed on a separate rubric or second page
Assuming you know what to do without checking for special rules
tips: Read the instructions twice, once for the big picture and once for details
If a rubric is provided, read it before you start so you know how you will be graded
Ask your teacher if any instruction is unclear, it is better to ask early
